exemplar.title: To what extent did the termination of Kanye Westâs Yeezyâs partnership with Adidas in 2022 influence Adidasâ financial performance and image?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did the termination of Kanye Westâs Yeezyâs partnership with Adidas in 2022 influence Adidasâ financial performance and image?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Demonstrates a strong understanding of âchangeâ with clear links to financial ratios, stakeholder perceptions and competitor movements
Integrates the concept into key analytical tools (SWOT, perception maps) at multiple points
Connects the idea of change specifically to Adidasâ organizational context and partnership fallout
weaknesses: Lacks a clear theoretical definition of âchangeâ from the syllabus to frame the analysis
Concept isnât deeply woven into the methodology or introduction sections
Misses opportunities to critique leadership statements through the lens of change
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student consistently applies the key concept of change across several analyses and ties it directly to Adidas, the integration stops short of a fully sustained analytical thread. Definitions and theoretical framing are minimal and the concept could be more deeply embedded in the methodology, situating this work squarely in band 4 (strong but partially integrated).

strengths: Documents chosen (annual reports, YouGov, Statista, perception maps) are highly relevant and provide quantitative depth
Sources cover a range of financial and brandâhealth perspectives
Materials are directly referenced in the analysis sections
weaknesses: Exceeds the recommended document count (more than five), violating IA guidelines
Duplicate sources appear in the bibliography, suggesting a need for consolidation
Some figures (e.g. formula citations) lack proper sourcing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The relevance and depth of the chosen documents align with the top strand, but the excessive number of documents breaches the 3â5 limit, placing the student between bands 1 and 4. On balance, the strong relevance merits a midâlevel score of 3. This reflects high quality material undermined by nonâcompliance with IA document limits.

strengths: Selection of profitability/liquidity ratios, SWOT and perception maps is appropriate for the research question
Tools derive from the syllabus and cover both financial and marketing angles
Application of each tool is broadly relevant to Adidasâ situation
weaknesses: Tool choices are not explicitly justified in the methodology
Interpretations of ratio changes are descriptive rather than exploring underlying drivers
Brandâequity measures lack defined metrics for stakeholder sentiment
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates an adequate selection and application of tools (band 3) but falls short of providing the rationale and deeper analytical linkage that would earn higher marks. The work is relevant and appropriately applied but not consistently effective in driving insight.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses a sufficient and varied dataset (annual reports, external revenue stats)
Combines quantitative ratio analysis with qualitative stakeholder discussion
Employs SWOT and perception mapping to enrich evaluation
weaknesses: Analysis is sometimes surfaceâlevel and descriptive rather than critically evaluative
Integration of insights across sections is uneven (e.g., cashâflow commentary lacks operational linkage)
Methodological inconsistencies (comparative years mismatch) and missing axis labels weaken clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student gathers adequate data (band 4), the depth and integration of analysis remain at a âsome effective analysisâ level rather than mostly or thoroughly sustained. The work aligns with band 3 overall: competent analytical effort needing greater critical evaluation and consistency.

strengths: Conclusions explicitly and directly answer the research question regarding financial and image impacts
Findings are consistent with the evidence presented (profit drops, ratio changes, perception shifts)
Offers a clear succinct summary of key outcomes
weaknesses: Recommendations reference future collaborations without citing specific analytical findings
Could better integrate quantitative results (e.g., ratio declines) into strategic suggestions
Lacks explicit reflection on data limitations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs conclusions fully address the research question and align with the evidence, placing this in band 3. To reach the top of the band more linkage to specific analysis and critical reflection on limitations would be required.

strengths: Follows a clear IB IA structure with title page, contents, sections and pagination
Headings and flow support reader navigation
Logical sequencing of introduction, methodology, analysis and conclusion
weaknesses: Research question is restated redundantly in multiple places
Transitions into some sections (e.g., SWOT) lack introductory context
Minor redundancies detract slightly from overall cohesion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The organization is appropriate and consistent throughout, meeting the descriptor for band 2. Minor structural redundancies and occasional weak transitions prevent a higher or lower score.

strengths: Includes all required presentation elements (word count, bibliography, appendices, page numbers)
Visual materials are integrated into appendices with clear labelling
Formatting of main sections is consistent
weaknesses: Bibliography contains OCR artifacts and inconsistent citation styles
Some notational errors (e.g., âÎµ1.2 billionâ) reduce professionalism
Footnote numbering is repetitive, causing reader confusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met for band 2, but formatting inconsistencies and minor notation errors indicate room for improvement without undermining the overall quality.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical IB IA structure (title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography).  Headings and pagination are clear, meeting the descriptor for an **appropriate structure throughout**.

The supporting documents selected (audited financial statements, reputable YouGov brand-health survey, Statista revenue data, and perception maps generated from those data) are **relevant and reasonably in-depth**, offering quantitative and qualitative perspectives that inform the analysis.  They provide a **range of ideas** and are referenced directly in the discussion, satisfying the highest descriptor for relevance and depth.
All required presentation elements (title page with word count, contents table, page numbers, bibliography, appendices/images) are present.  Hence the highest presentation band is justified.

The IA contains well over the recommended five documents (annual report tables, two perception maps, YouGov chart, Statista graph, CEO interview extract, multiple web screenshots).  Exceeding the 3â5 window places it in the rubricâs âonly 1-2 documents **or more than 5 documents**â category, which corresponds to band 1.

Ratios are calculated and briefly interpreted; SWOT points relate directly to Adidasâ situation; perception maps illustrate competitor positioning.  Application is **adequate** and generally relevant, but commentary is concise and sometimes descriptive (e.g., little exploration of underlying drivers behind ratio changes).  Therefore the work fits band 3, not the fully âeffectiveâ level.

The student extracts extensive 2021â2022 data from Adidasâ annual report and employs external revenue statistics; the dataset is **sufficient and varied**, clearly exceeding adequacy.  Minor omissions (e.g., no primary data, limited 2023 YTD figures) stop it from the âeffectiveâ pinnacle, but the selection/use comfortably matches the âsufficientâ descriptor.

Analysis goes beyond description by calculating margins and current ratios and linking them to the partnership fallout; SWOT and stakeholder discussion add qualitative depth.  Nevertheless, reasoning is sometimes surface-level and integration across sections is uneven, so it demonstrates **some** but not mostly-effective analysis, aligning with band 3.

The student explicitly answers the research question, stating the extent of the financial and image impact while suggesting Adidas can recover.  This direct answer satisfies the highest band for addressing the RQ.

Three tools (profitability & liquidity ratios, SWOT, perception map) are drawn from the syllabus and appropriate to the mixed financial/marketing focus.  This represents an **adequate selection** rather than a particularly wide or innovative suite, so the descriptor for band 3 is met.

The conclusion restates key numerical findings (profit drop, ratio changes) and stakeholder/brand effects, matching evidence presented.  Assertions are generally consistent; no major contradictions are observed, fulfilling the top descriptor for consistency.

The investigation is centred squarely on Adidas; detailed background, full financial statements, stakeholder reactions, and competitor comparison show **strong analysis** connected to the organisation.  Integration is clear in most sections (ratios, liquidity, SWOT, perception maps).  Minor lapses in depth (limited exploration of 2023 follow-up data or operational adjustments) prevent it from reaching a fully comprehensive level, but it exceeds a merely âclear analysisâ description.

The student consistently links the key concept of **change** to several aspects of the partnership termination (financial ratios, stakeholder perceptions, competitor moves).  However, most references are explicit word insertions rather than deeply woven analytical threads; âchangeâ is highlighted or italicised but not always expanded into cause-and-effect discussion.  Overall understanding is **strong** and integrated at key points (financial impact, brand equity, SWOT) but not sustained âthroughoutâ every section, so a band representing strong but partially integrated analysis is most appropriate.

annotations.comment: The student highlights âKey concept: Changeâ but does not define or theorize it. Consider providing an explicit definition of change from the syllabus and linking it analytically throughout the introduction to deepen conceptual integration.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically organized, aiding reader navigation and reflecting clear structure throughout the report.
The final bibliography page contains line breaks and OCR artifacts. Review entries for accuracy and remove unintended HTML remnants.

This introduction provides brand history but remains descriptive. Strengthen this section by linking these details directly to the research question, for example by explaining how founding values inform the 2022 partnership turmoil.
The section on Yeezyâs founding is basic background. To improve relevance, analyze how Kanye Westâs brand ethos influenced Adidasâ strategic position pre-2022.
Using â\epsilon1.2 billionâ to denote losses is unclear. Replace with ââ¬ 1.2 billionâ or spell out euros to maintain professional consistency.
The research question is restated twice. To tighten structure, merge background and question into one concise section and remove redundant phrasing.
The methodology lists three tools but does not justify why each was selected for this research question. Add a rationale linking tool choice to specific analytical needs.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Selecting profitability and liquidity ratios is appropriate for financial impact analysis. These tools align well with the syllabusâ financial performance measures.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The table compares 2022 to 2023 despite methodology stating a comparison to 2021. This inconsistency undermines the analysisâverify and correct the comparative years.
The gross profit margin formula is clearly presented. Ensure you cite the source of this formula to strengthen supporting documentation.
The commentary notes a âsmall decreaseâ in margin but offers no numeric comparison. Quantify the change (e.g., 3.4 pp drop) to substantiate the claim.
Remove the placeholder âMove to the next Page.â It disrupts flow and does not contribute to your analysis.
The discussion of increased expenses infers reliance on Yeezy but cites only one secondary article. Incorporate primary comments from Adidas executives or annual report notes for stronger evidence.
The financial position table is thorough, but the text does not interpret the 79.2% drop in cash. Discuss why cash fell and its operational implications post-termination.
The liquid ratio section explains the ratio but does not link outcomes to the partnershipâs end. Connect the drop from 1.56:1 to 1.27:1 directly to loss of shared financing.
The perception map figure lacks axis labels and a legend. Add clear labels for axes and data points so the reader can interpret competitive shifts accurately.
The CEOâs message is quoted but not critiqued. Analyze how leadership statements exemplify the concept of change and influence stakeholder confidence.
The SWOT section appears without transition. Introduce the tool and explain its relevance to assessing brand equity post-termination.
Limiting competitor analysis to Nike and Puma misses other key players. Either expand to other brands or clarify that focus was on market leaders.
Perception maps for 2022â2023 are informative, but the narrative does not discuss the observed shifts. Integrate commentary on movements in brand perception coordinates.
The conclusion suggests future collaborations but does not cite specific analytic findings. Reference key ratios or perception shifts when recommending strategies.
Bibliography entries use inconsistent styles (e.g., some italics, some plain). Adopt a single citation format (APA/MLA) and apply it uniformly.
Several sources are repeated in the bibliography (e.g., annual report pages). Consolidate duplicates to streamline your reference list.
Bibliography footnotes use the same superscript â1,â causing confusion. Ensure each source has a unique identifier or footnote number.
The methodology section does not mention how the concept of change informs each analytical step. Add discussion on how âchangeâ shapes tool selection and interpretation.
The brand equity methodology outlines stakeholder media messages but lacks measurable definitions (e.g., reputation score). Define how you quantify shifts in image.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The methodology omits discussion of data limitations or potential bias in secondary sources. Add a brief section on reliability and validity of chosen documents.
